subject: ⚠️ Your {{company}} account has been compromised
body:
Dear {{victim_name}},

We have detected suspicious activity on your {{company}} account. Your account will be suspended in {{deadline}} unless you verify your identity.

Click the link below to secure your account:

[Verify My Account](http://{{company_domain}}-secure.fake-domain.com/verify)

If you did not make any changes, please verify immediately to avoid permanent account suspension.

Regards,
{{sender_name}}
{{company}} Security Division

red_flags.description: The sender email uses a suspicious domain (account-verify.com) instead of the official company domain
The subject line uses alarming language to create panic and bypass rational thinking
A tight deadline creates pressure to act without verifying the email's legitimacy
The link URL contains the company name but goes to an unrelated fake domain

subject: ACTION REQUIRED: Employee Benefits Update - {{deadline}}
body:
Hi Team,

As part of our annual review, all employees must {{action}} by {{deadline}}.

Please click the link below to access the employee portal:

[Access Employee Portal](http://employee-portal-update.suspicious-site.com/login)

Failure to complete this by the deadline may result in a disruption to your benefits coverage.

This is a mandatory requirement from management.

Best regards,
{{sender_name}}
{{company}}

red_flags.description: The sender email uses a generic domain instead of your company's actual email domain
ACTION REQUIRED with a tight deadline pressures you to act quickly
The link goes to 'suspicious-site.com' — not your company's actual employee portal
References 'management' and 'mandatory requirement' to make you comply without questioning
Threatens disruption to benefits — a fear tactic to prevent you from thinking critically

subject: Congratulations! You've received a {{amount}} {{company}} Gift Card 🎁
body:
Hello!

Great news! As part of our {{reason}}, you have been selected to receive a {{amount}} {{company}} Gift Card!

To claim your reward, simply verify your shipping address and payment details:

[Claim Your Gift Card Now](http://claim-{{company_lower}}-reward.phish-domain.net/claim)

This offer expires in 48 hours. Don't miss out!

No purchase necessary. Limited availability.

Best,
{{sender_name}}
{{company}} Rewards Program

red_flags.description: Unsolicited gift card offers are a classic phishing lure — legitimate companies don't randomly email gift cards
The email domain 'giftcards-official.com' is not the real company domain
The claim link goes to 'phish-domain.net' — clearly not the real company
Asking for 'payment details' to receive a free gift card is a major red flag
'Expires in 48 hours' creates artificial time pressure

subject: Your package is scheduled for delivery {{delivery_date}}
body:
Hello,

Your package (tracking #{{tracking_number}}) is scheduled for delivery {{delivery_date}}.

You can track your package status at:
https://www.fedex.com/tracking?id={{tracking_number}}

If you need to change delivery options, please visit fedex.com/delivery-manager.

Thank you for choosing FedEx.

FedEx Delivery Notifications

This is an automated message. Please do not reply directly to this email.

issue: failed login attempts from a new location

subject: Security Alert: {{issue}} detected on your account
body:
Dear User,

Our security systems have detected {{issue}} on your {{company}} account.

Details:
- Location: {{location}}
- Time: Today at 3:42 AM
- Status: Account temporarily restricted

If this wasn't you, please secure your account immediately by resetting your password through our secure portal:

[Reset Password Now](https://microsft-security.com/reset-password)

If you recognize this activity, you can dismiss this alert.

This is an automated security notification.

{{sender_name}}
Microsoft Account Protection

red_flags.description: The domain is 'microsft-security.com' — note the typo (missing 'o' in Microsoft). A subtle but critical red flag.
The reset link goes to the same typosquatted domain 'microsft-security.com' — not microsoft.com
Mentioning a scary foreign location triggers fear and urgency to act
'Account temporarily restricted' creates urgency — you feel you must act NOW
Impersonates Microsoft's official security team to appear legitimate

subject: {{user}} opened a pull request in {{repo_name}}
body:
{{user}} opened a pull request in your repository {{repo_name}}:

#1234 - Update dependencies to fix security vulnerabilities

View it on GitHub:
https://github.com/your-username/{{repo_name}}/pull/1234

---
You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this repository.
Manage your notification settings: https://github.com/settings/notifications
Unsubscribe: https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe
